generatedSentence: A subtle tremor ran through the antiquated settlement on the east side of the raceway, a constant reminder of the Tyrant's iron grip. For an entire era, the populace had endured, longing for release from his majesty’s anathema. Hidden in a cool, ventilated library, a small group worked tirelessly. Their leader, a formidable woman known as "The Keyword," adjusted her keyboard, her forehead furrowed in deep thought. She wore simple jeans, but her eyes held the glint of knighthood, a promise of safety. Her mastery of ancient texts gave her leverage, while a cranky but loyal jack handled the complex routing of intelligence through a small, obdurate network of knickknacks and a ferryboat. The rebellion was implied, a template for freedom. The whereabouts of the Tyrant's inner circle were finally revealed after a major hack: a caravan of twinjets transporting an ultra-secret voucher through the rural forest. The plan was divvy up, a team would pass through the woods, while another, with a lucky charm shaped like a kiwi, would take to the skies. A visitor, known as "The Walker," a sapient being with funky styles, vouchsafed their support, promising to accommodate their wildest pranksters. The team, bestie to the cause, knew the risks; if they were involved, there was no back down. This was their chance to exorcize the devil of their dread, to prevent total damnation. The signal for liftoff was a single, jingled ball, whereupon their twinjets screeched into action, a style of combat unseen in ages. Below, the ground team, equipped with a taser and an umbrella, moved with kinky precision, avoiding the automated grinder bots. A tornado of resistance erupted as they engaged the Tyrant’s forces. The Tyrant's enforcers, like werewolves unleashed, were met with the rebels' fists. It was a high-stakes game of karma, a dental-grade extraction of power. The rebels, many feeling the burnout of years under the regime, knew this was the climax. They would unlock the central reserve, or pass away in the attempt, lest the Tyrant's reign be whelped anew. Through the chaos, a lantern shone, illuminating the Tyrant's panic room. He was feasting on tenderloin, fajitas, gnocchi, and candy, oblivious to the majority of his security being compromised. He had hoped his security jammer would hold, but the rebels' leverage was too great. The homely feeling of safety was gone. A single lamp was found, shining on his collection of gaudy jewelry. The rebel leader, now fully acknowledged for her knighthood, coolly took a keyword from his keyboard, a silent gesture that the old era was over. The Tyrant, in his last moments, knew he had been outmaneuvered. The sun rose on a new settlement, free from his wrath, offering a kinder, happier future for all.
panelSentences: A subtle tremor ran through the antiquated settlement on the east side of the raceway, a constant reminder of the Tyrant's iron grip. For an entire era, the populace had endured, longing for release from his majesty’s anathema. Hidden in a cool, ventilated library, a small group worked tirelessly. Their leader, a formidable woman known as "The Keyword," adjusted her keyboard, her forehead furrowed in deep thought. She wore simple jeans, but her eyes held the glint of knighthood, a promise of safety. Her mastery of ancient texts gave her leverage, while a cranky but loyal jack handled the complex routing of intelligence through a small, obdurate network of knickknacks and a ferryboat.

The rebellion was implied, a template for freedom. The whereabouts of the Tyrant's inner circle were finally revealed after a major hack: a caravan of twinjets transporting an ultra-secret voucher through the rural forest. The plan was divvy up, a team would pass through the woods, while another, with a lucky charm shaped like a kiwi, would take to the skies. A visitor, known as "The Walker," a sapient being with funky styles, vouchsafed their support, promising to accommodate their wildest pranksters. The team, bestie to the cause, knew the risks; if they were involved, there was no back down. This was their chance to exorcize the devil of their dread, to prevent total damnation.

The signal for liftoff was a single, jingled ball, whereupon their twinjets screeched into action, a style of combat unseen in ages. Below, the ground team, equipped with a taser and an umbrella, moved with kinky precision, avoiding the automated grinder bots. A tornado of resistance erupted as they engaged the Tyrant’s forces. The Tyrant's enforcers, like werewolves unleashed, were met with the rebels' fists. It was a high-stakes game of karma, a dental-grade extraction of power. The rebels, many feeling the burnout of years under the regime, knew this was the climax. They would unlock the central reserve, or pass away in the attempt, lest the Tyrant's reign be whelped anew.

Through the chaos, a lantern shone, illuminating the Tyrant's panic room. He was feasting on tenderloin, fajitas, gnocchi, and candy, oblivious to the majority of his security being compromised. He had hoped his security jammer would hold, but the rebels' leverage was too great. The homely feeling of safety was gone. A single lamp was found, shining on his collection of gaudy jewelry. The rebel leader, now fully acknowledged for her knighthood, coolly took a keyword from his keyboard, a silent gesture that the old era was over. The Tyrant, in his last moments, knew he had been outmaneuvered. The sun rose on a new settlement, free from his wrath, offering a kinder, happier future for all.
rawPanelSentences: A subtle tremor ran through the antiquated settlement on the east side of the raceway, a constant reminder of the Tyrant's iron grip. For an entire era, the populace had endured, longing for release from his majesty’s anathema. Hidden in a cool, ventilated library, a small group worked tirelessly. Their leader, a formidable woman known as "The Keyword," adjusted her keyboard, her forehead furrowed in deep thought. She wore simple jeans, but her eyes held the glint of knighthood, a promise of safety. Her mastery of ancient texts gave her leverage, while a cranky but loyal jack handled the complex routing of intelligence through a small, obdurate network of knickknacks and a ferryboat.

The rebellion was implied, a template for freedom. The whereabouts of the Tyrant's inner circle were finally revealed after a major hack: a caravan of twinjets transporting an ultra-secret voucher through the rural forest. The plan was divvy up, a team would pass through the woods, while another, with a lucky charm shaped like a kiwi, would take to the skies. A visitor, known as "The Walker," a sapient being with funky styles, vouchsafed their support, promising to accommodate their wildest pranksters. The team, bestie to the cause, knew the risks; if they were involved, there was no back down. This was their chance to exorcize the devil of their dread, to prevent total damnation.

The signal for liftoff was a single, jingled ball, whereupon their twinjets screeched into action, a style of combat unseen in ages. Below, the ground team, equipped with a taser and an umbrella, moved with kinky precision, avoiding the automated grinder bots. A tornado of resistance erupted as they engaged the Tyrant’s forces. The Tyrant's enforcers, like werewolves unleashed, were met with the rebels' fists. It was a high-stakes game of karma, a dental-grade extraction of power. The rebels, many feeling the burnout of years under the regime, knew this was the climax. They would unlock the central reserve, or pass away in the attempt, lest the Tyrant's reign be whelped anew.

Through the chaos, a lantern shone, illuminating the Tyrant's panic room. He was feasting on tenderloin, fajitas, gnocchi, and candy, oblivious to the majority of his security being compromised. He had hoped his security jammer would hold, but the rebels' leverage was too great. The homely feeling of safety was gone. A single lamp was found, shining on his collection of gaudy jewelry. The rebel leader, now fully acknowledged for her knighthood, coolly took a keyword from his keyboard, a silent gesture that the old era was over. The Tyrant, in his last moments, knew he had been outmaneuvered. The sun rose on a new settlement, free from his wrath, offering a kinder, happier future for all.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined female leader with a cybernetic arm and a flowing, dark-red trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a dimly lit, futuristic library, hunched over a glowing keyboard, surrounded by holographic schematics, focused medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist leading a small, elite team through a dense, bioluminescent forest at night, a sleek twinjet taking off in the background, wide-angle shot from a low perspective. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist engaged in close-quarters combat against a robotic enforcer, her cybernetic arm glowing, a taser arcing, dynamic action shot from a slightly elevated angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist standing victorious over a deactivated Tyrant, a gentle light illuminating the scene through a shattered skylight, a sense of relief and hope in her expression, eye-level medium shot.

definitions.definition: A shake, quiver, or vibration.
definitions.example: She felt a tremor in her stomach before going on stage.

definitions.definition: An earthquake.
definitions.example: Did you feel the tremor this morning?

definitions.definition: To shake or quiver excessively and rapidly or involuntarily; to tremble.
A usurper; one who gains power and rules extralegally, distinguished from kings elevated by election or succession.
Any monarch or governor.
A despot; a ruler who governs unjustly, cruelly, or harshly.
(by extension) Any person who abuses the power of position or office to treat others unjustly, cruelly, or harshly.
(by extension) A villain; a person or thing who uses strength or violence to treat others unjustly, cruelly, or harshly.
The tyrant bird, members of the family Tyrannidae, which often fight or drive off other birds which approach their nests.
To act like a tyrant; to be tyrannical.
To tyrannize.
Tyrannical, tyrannous; like, characteristic of, or in the manner of a tyrant.
An aircraft powered by two jet engines.
Two jets of particles from an event or source
Any word used as the key to a code.
Any word used in a reference work to link to other words or other information.
A reserved word used to identify a specific command, function etc.
Any word that occurs in a text more often than normal.
To tag with keywords, as for example to facilitate searching.
(etc.) A set of keys used to operate a typewriter, computer etc.
A component of many instruments including the piano, organ, and harpsichord consisting of usually black and white keys that cause different tones to be produced when struck.
A device with keys of a musical keyboard, used to control electronic sound-producing devices which may be built into or separate from the keyboard device.
To type on a computer keyboard.
definitions.example: Keyboarding is the part of this job I hate the most.

definitions.definition: An honour whereby one is made into a knight, and one can thereafter be called "Sir"
definitions.example: He's got an OBE, and MBE and his recent work should entitle him to a knighthood.

definitions.definition: The quality of being a knight.
The knights collectively, the body of knights.
The quality of being impressive and great.
definitions.example: the majesty of the Great Pyramids

definitions.definition: A person who is transformed or can transform into a wolf or a wolflike human, often said to transform during a full moon.
The condition or feeling of being safe; security; certainty.
definitions.example: If you push it to the limit, safety is not guaranteed.

definitions.definition: A mechanism on a weapon or dangerous equipment designed to prevent accidental firing.
definitions.example: Be sure that the safety is set before proceeding.

definitions.definition: An instance of a player being sacked or tackled in the end zone, or stepping out of the end zone and off the field, resulting in two points to the opposite team.
definitions.example: He sacked the quarterback in the end zone for a safety.

definitions.definition: Any of the defensive players who are in position furthest from the line of scrimmage and whose responsibility is to defend against passes as well as to be the tacklers of last resort.
definitions.example: The free safety made a game-saving tackle on the runner who had broken past the linebackers.

definitions.definition: A safety squeeze.
Preservation from escape; close custody.
A safety bicycle.
To secure (a mechanical component, as in aviation) to keep it from becoming detached even under vibration.
To secure a firing pin, as in guns, to keep the gun from firing
The replacement of stale or noxious air with fresh.
The mechanical system used to circulate and replace air.
An exchange of views during a discussion.
The public exposure of an issue or topic.
The bodily process of breathing; the inhalation of air to provide oxygen, and the exhalation of spent air to remove carbon dioxide.
The mechanical system used to assist breathing.
Collectively, personal ornamentation such as rings, necklaces, brooches and bracelets, made of precious metals and sometimes set with gemstones.
definitions.example: She had more jewellery ornamented about her than any three ladies needed.

definitions.definition: A coarse mediaeval coat of defence, especially one made of leather.
Weak, unwell.
(of a machine, etc.) Not in good working condition.
Grouchy, grumpy, irritable; easily upset.
definitions.example: He got home from a long day at work tired and cranky.

definitions.definition: Not in perfect mental working order; eccentric, peculiar.
Full of spirit; spirited.
An institution which holds books and/or other forms of media for use by the public or qualified people often lending them out, as well as providing various other services for its users.
(by extension) Any institution that lends out its goods for use by the public or a community.
A collection of books or other forms of stored information.
An equivalent collection of analogous information in a non-printed form, e.g. record library.
A room dedicated to storing books.
A collection of software routines that provide functionality to be incorporated into or used by a computer program.
A collection of DNA material from a single organism or relative to a single disease.
The deck or draw pile.
A force compounded by means of a lever rotating around a pivot; see torque.
definitions.example: A crowbar uses leverage to pry nails out of wood.

definitions.definition: By extension, any influence which is compounded or used to gain an advantage.
definitions.example: Try using competitors’ prices for leverage in the negotiation.

definitions.definition: The use of borrowed funds with a contractually determined return to increase the ability of a business to invest and earn an expected higher return, but usually at high risk.
definitions.example: Leverage is great until something goes wrong with your investments and you still have to pay your debts.

definitions.definition: The ability to earn very high returns when operating at high capacity utilization of a facility.
definitions.example: Their variable-cost-reducing investments have dramatically increased their leverage.

definitions.definition: To use; to exploit; to manipulate in order to take full advantage (of something).
definitions.example: They plan to leverage off the publicity to get a good distribution agreement.

definitions.definition: The experience of long-term exhaustion and diminished interest, especially in one's career.
A marijuana addict; one whose brains have been burned out.
The shutoff of a rocket motor following the complete exhaustion of its fuel supply, or having been irreversibly throttled after the application of a planned delta-v.
The failure of an electrical device, usually through overheating due to the application of excessive power.
Using the throttle to spin the wheels of a vehicle being held stationary, causing the spinning tires to produce smoke and burn rubber.
Lacking in beauty or elegance, plain in appearance, physically unattractive.
Cozy, befitting a home.
Characteristic of or belonging to home; domestic.
On intimate or friendly terms with (someone); familiar; at home (with a person); intimate.
(of animals) Domestic; tame.
Personal; private.
Friendly; kind; gracious; cordial.
Conservative and family-oriented.
definitions.example: I am seeking a beautiful homely girl for marriage.

definitions.definition: Simple; plain; familiar; unelaborate; unadorned.
definitions.example: a homely garment

definitions.definition: The position or authority of a master; dominion; command; supremacy; superiority.
Superiority in war or competition; victory; triumph; preeminence.
Contest for superiority.
A masterly operation; a feat.
The philosopher's stone.
The act or process of mastering; the state of having mastered; expertise.
More than half (50%) of some group.
definitions.example: The majority agreed that the new proposal was the best.

definitions.definition: The difference between the winning vote and the rest of the votes.
definitions.example: The winner with 53% had a 6% majority over the loser with 47%.

definitions.definition: Legal adulthood.
definitions.example: By the time I reached my majority, I had already been around the world twice.

definitions.definition: The office held by a member of the armed forces in the rank of major.
definitions.example: On receiving the news of his promotion, Charles Snodgrass said he was delighted to be entering his majority.

definitions.definition: Ancestors; ancestry.
Location; where something is situated.
definitions.example: The whereabouts of the escaped snake are unknown.

definitions.definition: In, at or near what location
definitions.example: Whereabouts do you live?

definitions.definition: A ban or curse pronounced with religious solemnity by ecclesiastical authority, often accompanied by excommunication; something denounced as accursed.
(by extension) Something which is vehemently disliked by somebody.
An imprecation; a curse; a malediction.
Any person or thing anathematized, or cursed by ecclesiastical authority.
To issue a formal command.
To earnestly appeal to or advise; to charge solemnly.
definitions.example: Party members are adjured to promote awareness of this problem.

definitions.definition: A moderate or refreshing state of cold; moderate temperature of the air between hot and cold; coolness.
definitions.example: in the cool of the morning

definitions.definition: A calm temperament.
The property of being cool, popular or in fashion.
Having a slightly low temperature; mildly or pleasantly cold.
Allowing or suggesting heat relief.

definitions.definition: Of a color, in the range of violet to green.
definitions.example: If you have a reddish complexion, you should mainly wear cool colors.

definitions.definition: Of a person, not showing emotion; calm and in control of oneself.
Unenthusiastic, lukewarm, skeptical.
definitions.example: His proposals had a cool reception.

definitions.definition: Calmly audacious.
definitions.example: In control as always, he came up with a cool plan.

definitions.definition: Applied facetiously to a sum of money, commonly as if to give emphasis to the largeness of the amount.
Of a person, knowing what to do and how to behave; considered popular by others.
In fashion, part of or fitting the in crowd; originally hipster slang.
Of an action, all right; acceptable; that does not present a problem.
definitions.example: Is it cool if I sleep here tonight?

definitions.definition: Of a person, not upset by circumstances that might ordinarily be upsetting.
definitions.example: I'm completely cool with my girlfriend leaving me.

definitions.definition: Quietly impudent, defiant, or selfish; deliberately presuming: said of persons and acts.
One who grinds something, such as the teeth.
(anatomical) A molar.
A power tool with a spinning abrasive disc, used for grinding, smoothing, and shaping materials, usually metal.
A sandwich made on a long, cylindrical roll.
definitions.example: I am going to the deli to get a grinder for lunch.

definitions.definition: A kitchen gadget for processing coffee, herbs etc. into small or powdered pieces
The restless flycatcher (Myiagra inquieta) of Australia, which makes a noise like a scissors grinder.
A fan or performer of grindcore music.
A biohacker who uses cybernetic implants or biochemicals to enhance or change their own body.
A student who studies hard; a swot.
A person who coaches students for an upcoming examination.

A low-ranking attorney with no clients who works very hard.
An outdoor space for drills and parades.
A violent windstorm characterized by a mobile, twisting, funnel-shaped cloud.

definitions.definition: A small ornament of minor value.

A Tex-Mex dish of strips of spicy marinated meat and/or vegetables in a soft flour tortilla, often served with salad or a savoury filling.

An instrument for effecting a purpose (either literally or figuratively); a tool, or an opportunity or pretext.
The gross amount of wagering within a given period of time or for a given event at one of more establishments.

A name, nickname or pseudonym.
A title attached to one's name, such as Doctor or Colonel.

definitions.definition: A reference to an object or structure that can be stored in a variable.

A half-gallon (1.75-liter) bottle of alcohol. (Called a sixty in Canada.)
A point, an extremity of land.

A variety of shoe, typically made of heavy leather.
(by ellipsis) An Oxford Dictionary.
The act of settling.
The state of being settled.
A colony that is newly established; a place or region newly settled.
A community of people living together, such as a hamlet, village, town, or city.
The gradual sinking of a building. Fractures or dislocations caused by settlement.
The delivery of goods by the seller and payment for them by the buyer, under a previously agreed trade or transaction or contract entered into.
A disposition of property, or the act of granting it.
A settled place of abode; residence; a right growing out of legal residence.
A resolution of a dispute.
A dividend; a share or portion.
To divide into portions.
definitions.example: We divvied the money into equal portions to split between us.

definitions.definition: The event of setting (someone or something) free (e.g. hostages, slaves, prisoners, caged animals, hooked or stuck mechanisms).
The distribution of an initial or new and upgraded version of a computer software product; the distribution can be either public or private.
Anything recently released or made available (as for sale).

definitions.definition: The giving up of a claim, especially a debt.
Liberation from pain or suffering.
The process by which a chemical substance is set free.
(sound synthesis) The act or manner of ending a sound.
In the block system, a printed card conveying information and instructions to be used at intermediate sidings without telegraphic stations.
A device adapted to hold or release a device or mechanism as required.
To let go (of); to cease to hold or contain.
definitions.example: He released his grasp on the lever.

definitions.definition: To make available to the public.
definitions.example: They released the new product later than intended.

definitions.definition: To free or liberate; to set free.
definitions.example: He was released after two years in prison.

definitions.definition: To discharge.
definitions.example: They released thousands of gallons of water into the river each month.

definitions.definition: (of a call) To hang up.
definitions.example: If you continue to use abusive language, I will need to release the call.

definitions.definition: To let go, as a legal claim; to discharge or relinquish a right to, as lands or tenements, by conveying to another who has some right or estate in possession, as when the person in remainder releases his right to the tenant in possession; to quit.

A macromolecule which provides a pattern for the synthesis of another molecule.

To strike verbally or gesturally with ill intents.
definitions.example: As I spoke too loudly during the solemn church service, my husband shot me a glance that emotionally tasered me to the point of silence until we returned home 2 hours later.

definitions.definition: A member of the family Psychidae of the Lepidoptera.
Eastern tent caterpillar.
Fall webworm.
Full of kinks; liable to kink or curl.

definitions.definition: Marked by unconventional sexual preferences or behavior, as fetishism, sadomasochism, and other sexual practices.
Queer; eccentric; crotchety.

definitions.example: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!

definitions.definition: Generally, anything that provides protection.
definitions.example: The fighters provide a defensive air umbrella over the battle group.

definitions.definition: Something that covers a wide range of concepts, purposes, groups, etc.
definitions.example: The test facility was established under the umbrella of the company's quality program.

definitions.definition: The main body of a jellyfish, excluding the tentacles.
definitions.example: Jellyfish are composed of more than 90% water and most of their umbrella mass is made up of gelatinous material.

definitions.definition: A sunfish.
(of weather or a day) Featuring a lot of sunshine.

definitions.definition: (of a place) Receiving a lot of sunshine.
definitions.example: I would describe Spain as sunny, but it's nothing in comparison to the Sahara.

definitions.definition: (of a person or a person's mood) cheerful

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the sun; proceeding from, or resembling the sun; shiny; radiant.
Sunny side up
A category into which sexually-reproducing organisms are divided on the basis of their reproductive roles in their species.
definitions.example: The effect of the medication is dependent upon age, sex, and other factors.

The members of such a category, taken collectively.
The distinction and relation between these categories, especially in humans; gender.

Genitalia: a penis or vagina.

Someone who pays a visit to a specific place or event; a sightseer or tourist.
(usually in the plural) Someone, or a team, that is playing away from home.

An object which lands or passes by Earth or its orbit.
A head or overseer of an institution such as a college (in which case, equivalent to the university's chancellor) or cathedral or hospital, who resolves disputes, gives ceremonial speeches, etc.

To die.

An evil creature.
(with article) The chief devil; Satan.

A fictional image of a man, usually red or orange in skin color; with a set of horns on his head, a pointed goatee and a long tail and carrying a pitchfork; that represents evil and portrayed to children in an effort to discourage bad behavior.
The bad part of the conscience; the opposite to the angel.
definitions.example: The devil in me wants to let him suffer.

definitions.definition: A wicked or naughty person, or one who harbors reckless, spirited energy, especially in a mischievous way; usually said of a young child.
definitions.example: Those two kids are devils in a toy store.

definitions.definition: A thing that is awkward or difficult to understand or do.
definitions.example: That math problem was a devil.

definitions.definition: (with an article, as an intensifier) Hell.
definitions.example: She is having a devil of a time fixing it.

definitions.definition: A person, especially a man; used to express a particular opinion of him, usually in the phrases poor devil and lucky devil.
A dust devil.
(Christian Science) An evil or erring entity.
(in compounds) A barren, unproductive and unused area.
definitions.example: devil strip

definitions.definition: A dish, as a bone with the meat, broiled and excessively peppered; a grill with Cayenne pepper.
A machine for tearing or cutting rags, cotton, etc.
A Tasmanian devil.
An endurance event where riders who fall behind are periodically eliminated.
To make like a devil; to invest with the character of a devil.
To annoy or bother.
To work as a ‘devil’; to work for a lawyer or writer without fee or recognition.
To prepare (food) with spices, making it spicy:
The state of being damned; condemnation; openly expressed disapprobation.
Condemnation to everlasting punishment in the future state, or the punishment itself.
Great fear in view of impending evil; fearful apprehension of danger; anticipatory terror.
definitions.example: my visit to the doctor is filling me with dread

definitions.definition: Reverential or respectful fear; awe.
Somebody or something dreaded.
A person highly revered.
Fury; dreadfulness.
A Rastafarian.
(chiefly in the plural) dreadlock
To fear greatly.
To anticipate with fear.

definitions.definition: To be in dread, or great fear.
To style (the hair) into dreadlocks.
Terrible; greatly feared.
Awe-inspiring; held in fearful awe.
Someone or something that reminds.

definitions.definition: Writing that reminds of open payments.
definitions.example: She ignored first the reminder of 80 cents. At the end, she was sentenced to pay 200 euros!

definitions.definition: A convoy or procession of travelers, their vehicles and cargo, and any pack animals, especially camels crossing a desert.
A furnished vehicle towed behind a car, etc., and used as a dwelling when stationary.
To travel in a caravan (procession).

definitions.definition: To travel and/or live in a caravan (vehicle).
definitions.example: When my parents retired they really got back into caravanning.

definitions.definition: The point in the launch of a rocket or an aircraft where it leaves contact with the ground.
The point at which a person or animal leaves the ground, as for example when jumping.
To execute (somebody) without a proper legal trial or procedure, especially by hanging and backed by a mob.
The tenderest part of a loin of meat, especially of pork or beef.
A district of a city where corruption is common, often because the district is devoted to questionable businesses (peep shows, etc) which are easy for police to blackmail and extort.
To take a less aggressive position in a conflict than one previously has or has planned to.

definitions.definition: Best friend
The sum total of a person's actions, which determine the person's next incarnation in samsara, the cycle of death and rebirth.
A force or law of nature which causes one to reap what one sows; destiny; fate.
A distinctive feeling, aura, or atmosphere.
(Reddit) A score assigned to a user of a discussion forum, indicating the popularity of their posts with other users.
To roll or fold up; to wind round; to entwine.
To envelop completely; to surround; to cover; to hide.

definitions.definition: To complicate or make intricate, as in grammatical structure.
To connect with something as a natural or logical consequence or effect; to include necessarily; to imply.
To take in; to gather in; to mingle confusedly; to blend or merge.
To envelop, enfold, entangle.

definitions.definition: To engage (someone) to participate in a task.

definitions.definition: To raise to any assigned power; to multiply, as a quantity, into itself a given number of times.
definitions.example: a quantity involved to the third or fourth power

definitions.definition: Complicated.

definitions.definition: Associated with others, be a participant or make someone be a participant (in a crime, process, etc.)
definitions.example: He got involved in a bar fight.

definitions.definition: Having an affair with someone.
To do a specific task by employing physical or mental powers.

definitions.definition: To effect by gradual degrees.
definitions.example: he worked his way through the crowd

definitions.definition: To embroider with thread.
To set into action.

definitions.definition: To cause to ferment.
To ferment.
To exhaust, by working.

definitions.definition: To shape, form, or improve a material.

definitions.definition: To operate in a certain place, area, or speciality.

definitions.definition: To operate in or through; as, to work the phones.
To provoke or excite; to influence.

definitions.definition: To use or manipulate to one’s advantage.

definitions.definition: To cause to happen or to occur as a consequence.

definitions.definition: To cause to work.

definitions.definition: To function correctly; to act as intended; to achieve the goal designed for.

definitions.definition: To influence.
definitions.example: They worked on her to join the group.

definitions.definition: To effect by gradual degrees; as, to work into the earth.
To move in an agitated manner.

definitions.definition: To behave in a certain way when handled
definitions.example: this dough does not work easily;  the soft metal works well

definitions.definition: (with two objects) To cause (someone) to feel (something); to do unto somebody (something, whether good or bad).
To hurt; to ache.
Designed or executed in a particular manner or to a particular degree.
Wrought.
Prepared so as to demonstrate the steps required.

A walking frame.

A zombie.

Any part of something that is smaller or slimmer than the rest, now usually with anatomical reference to the back.
To make little or less.
To become small; to dwindle.
Not large or big; insignificant; few in number.

In or into small pieces.
To a small extent.
In a low tone; softly.
To make a noise of metal or glass clattering against itself.

definitions.definition: To rhyme or sound with a jingling effect.
Any device used to jam radio reception.

To change place.

To move through time.
To be accepted.
In any game, to decline to play in one's turn.
To do or be better.
To take heed.
A medical doctor who specializes in dentistry.
A person from the countryside; a rustic.
Relating to the countryside or to agriculture.
Edible, sweet-tasting confectionery containing sugar, or sometimes artificial sweeteners, and often flavored with fruit, chocolate, nuts, herbs and spices, or artificial flavors.
A piece of confectionery of this kind.
(slang, chiefly US) crack cocaine.
To cook in, or coat with, sugar syrup.
To have sugar crystals form in or on.

definitions.definition: To be formed into candy; to solidify in a candylike form or mass.
Italian pasta-like dumplings made of potato or semolina.
To drive out supposed evil spirits from a person, place or thing, especially by an incantation or prayer

definitions.definition: Producing, or resulting in, good fortune

definitions.definition: Having a benevolent, courteous, friendly, generous, gentle, liberal, sympathetic, or warm-hearted nature or disposition, marked by consideration for – and service to – others.
Affectionate.

definitions.definition: Favorable.

definitions.definition: To hack into; to gain unauthorized access to (a computer system, e.g., a website, or network) by manipulating code.
